article_title: Executive Committee of Council Meeting — July 07, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby School District community schools overview presentation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a presentation from the Burnaby School District on their community schools program. This is an invited overview, not a final decision. The presentation will explain how community schools operate and what services they offer to local residents and families. No vote on a bylaw or policy change is expected at this time — this is an information item for Council.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
5.1 INVITED PRESENTATION: BURNABY SCHOOL DISTRICT - COMMUNITY SCHOOLS OVERVIEW
Presenter:
Sherri Brattston, District Principal
agenda_briefs.article_title: Executive Committee of Council Meeting Agenda — July 07, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Arts Council delegation to Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will hear a delegation from the Burnaby Arts Council on July 7, 2026. Sabrina Modder, President, will present on the BAC's renewal process since 2024, operational sustainability, partnerships, and expanding arts in Burnaby. The delegation requests continued operational funding from the City. No decision is being made at this meeting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Arts Council delegation to Executive Committee

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $25,000 operating grant for Burnaby Arts Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a proposal to award the Burnaby Arts Council $25,000 in annual operating funding for the 2026 fiscal year. The funding ensures the Arts Council remains eligible for provincial grants (e.g., BC Arts Council, $17,300 confirmed) and supports general operating expenses, contract expertise, and limited public activities. The Arts Council's 2026 provisional budget shows total revenues of $71,580 and expenses of $71,544.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Burnaby Arts Council operating grant

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Festivals Burnaby Grant Program for July 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a $1,000 Festivals Burnaby Grant to the Tanzanian Society of BC for the TanBC Community Cultural Festival on September 21, 2026 at Confederation Park. The grant covers programming and venue costs. The program has $114,100 remaining in its $250,000 annual budget. Eligible applicants are local/regional non-profits producing festivals in Burnaby; small/new events are accepted year-round at least 3 months before the event.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you operate a non-profit festival in Burnaby, apply for a Small/New event grant at least 3 months before your event date.

key_dates.label: Council decision on July 2026 grant recommendation

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on July 2026 community grant recipient
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will vote on a staff recommendation to award a $7,500 Initiative grant to CTS Youth Society for Free Summer Youth Outdoor Camps running June 18–August 18, 2026. The camps serve an average of 24 youth per camp at $325 per youth, and the grant amount matches the $7,500 received in 2024 and 2025. The Community Grants Program has $113,107.62 remaining from a $430,000 budget as of July 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Council vote on July 2026 community grant recommendation
CTS Youth Society free summer camps run
CTS Youth Society free summer camps end

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review Community Schools funding and programs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received an information report on Burnaby's eight Community Schools (Lochdale, Edmonds, Gilmore, Maywood, Second Street, Stoney Creek, Stride Avenue elementary and Byrne Creek secondary). The City's $618,970 annual contribution for 2025-2026 was approved on June 2, 2026, covering coordinators and operational costs. The School District contributes $984,355. Programs include early learning, out-of-school time, youth connections, and family/community support, with summer programs at 11 schools in July-August 2026 serving over 1,100 students.

key_dates.label: Community School summer programs run — July-August 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to receive MOSAIC thank-you letter
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a thank-you letter to MOSAIC for their support. This is a routine acknowledgment item on the agenda. No new rules, fees, or requirements are proposed. The letter simply thanks MOSAIC for their work. There is no bylaw or policy change being voted on.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Rowing BC sport hosting grant
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from Rowing BC for an off-cycle Sport Hosting Grant of up to $7,500 to help host the 2026 Provincial Championships at Burnaby Lake. The request is due to a 28% increase in the cost of renting the Burnaby Lake course, which was announced after the event budget and registration fees were set. The event attracts participants from BC, Alberta, Washington State, and California.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to discuss personal info for municipal award or honour
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a closed-session item that would allow them to discuss personal information about an individual being considered for a municipal award or honour. The meeting will be closed to the public under sections 90 and 92 of the Community Charter, which let councils exclude the public when discussing private details about a person. This is a proposed step — no decision has been made yet on who the award or honour might go to.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
10. CLOSED
Public excluded according to Sections 90 and 92 of the
Community Charter
for the Committee to consider matters concerning:
90(1)(b) personal information about an identifiable individual who is being considered for a municipal award or honour, or who has offered to provide a gift to the municipality on condition of anonymity.
